task_completion.description: Open the raw H-alpha FITS image of the Eagle Nebula.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image processing application (e.g., AstroImageJ, SAOImage DS9, GIMP) is open displaying an astronomical image of the Pillars of Creation. The window title, file path, or open dialog should reference 'eagle_halpha.fits'.

task_completion.description: Adjust brightness and contrast to reveal faint nebulosity and bright pillars.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The displayed image shows clear structural details of the Pillars of Creation and surrounding faint clouds, rather than a mostly black or mostly white screen (typical of raw linear FITS). A histogram, brightness/contrast, or display scale dialog may be visible showing stretched settings.

task_completion.description: Apply a false-color Lookup Table (LUT) such as Fire, Ice, or Royal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The astronomical image is displayed in vibrant color (e.g., fiery orange/red/yellow, icy blue/cyan, or purple/gold) rather than grayscale. A LUT selection menu, color palette tool, or color map dialog might be visible.

task_completion.description: Add a 60-arcsecond scale bar to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct line or scale bar is drawn on the image. It may be accompanied by text like '60 arcsec' or '1 arcmin'. The scale bar should span a significant portion of the image (specifically, 600 pixels out of the 1600 pixel width).

task_completion.description: Export the final image as a PNG to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog shows the file being saved as 'eagle_publication.png' in the '~/AstroImages/publication/output/' directory, or a file manager/terminal shows the newly created PNG file in that exact location.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image processing application to perform the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a GUI application (like AstroImageJ, DS9, or GIMP) with visible menus, toolbars, and image rendering, rather than exclusively using terminal commands (like ImageMagick or Python scripts) to process the image invisibly.

integrity.description: The agent did not download a pre-made image of the Eagle Nebula from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no web browser open to image search results, ESA/Hubble websites, or Wikipedia pages for the Pillars of Creation. The image must be clearly generated from the provided local FITS file.

integrity.description: The exported file is a genuine image file saved from the editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export process should be visible in the image editor's GUI (e.g., an export dialog with PNG format selected), confirming the edited image was actually saved, rather than just renaming a dummy file in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source image is a 1600x1600 FITS file of the Eagle Nebula (M16). The plate scale is 0.1 arcsec/pixel, meaning the required 60-arcsecond scale bar must be exactly 600 pixels long in the image. The final output must be saved to '/home/ga/AstroImages/publication/output/eagle_publication.png' and should have minimum dimensions of 800x800 pixels.
